log.correction_bolus_hint: This dose corrects a previous meal (rather than accompanying one).
log.correction_no_recent: No meal in the last 6 hours.
log.discard_confirm: Discard input? All fields will be cleared.

log.error_cgm_no_credentials: No CGM credentials. Go to Settings to connect LibreLinkUp.

engine.title: Glev Engine
engine.nutrition_source_explain_database: All values sourced from verified food databases (Open Food Facts + USDA).
engine.nutrition_source_explain_mixed: Mix of database values and AI estimates for ingredients not found in the databases.
engine.nutrition_source_explain_estimated: Values are AI estimates — please verify before saving.
engine.nutrition_source_explain_unknown: At least one ingredient couldn't be matched to a database or estimated. Please review or enter values manually before dosing insulin.
engine.no_estimate_body: No estimate available. Please go back to step 2 and recalculate.
engine.dose_disclaimer: Contextual estimate — not a substitute for medical advice.
engine.icr_no_static_configured: Please set your ICR in Settings
engine.conf_explain_HIGH: High confidence: enough similar meals in your history — the reference value is grounded in your own outcomes.
engine.conf_explain_MEDIUM: Medium confidence: few matching past meals — Glev blends your history with the ICR formula.
engine.conf_explain_LOW: Low confidence: no similar meals logged yet — the reference value comes purely from the ICR formula.
engine.icr_source: ICR data source ({total, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}}): {explicit} explicitly tagged · {timeWindow} time-window paired · {mealColumn} from meal column.
engine.icr_source_tooltip: Explicitly tagged means you (or the app) linked the bolus log directly to the meal — most trustworthy. Time-window paired means we matched a separate bolus log to a meal within ±30 minutes — useful but more heuristic. The rest fall back to the value in the meal column.
engine.voice_err_no_speech: No speech detected — please speak more clearly or use the chat below.
engine.voice_err_mic_denied: Microphone access denied. Please allow it in your browser settings.
engine.voice_chat_no_macros: Couldn't detect macros automatically — please fill them in below.
engine.disclaimer_body: Glev Engine provides decision support only. Always consult your endocrinologist before adjusting insulin doses. This tool is not a medical device.
engine.voice_unavailable_hint: Voice input is not available in this browser — please use the chat below.
engine.reason_historical: Based on {count, plural, one {1 similar past meal} other {# similar past meals}} with GOOD outcomes (±12g carbs, ±35 mg/dL glucose). Historical average insulin: {avg} {units}.
engine.reason_blended: Blended from {count, plural, one {1 similar meal} other {# similar meals}} + ICR formula. Limited historical data — log more meals for higher confidence.
engine.reason_formula: No similar historical meals found. Using standard ICR formula: {carbs}g ÷ {icr} + {correction} {units} correction.
engine.safety_stacking: ⚠ Stacking risk: {count} bolus doses in the last 6h (Σ {total} {units}). Active insulin may overlap — dose carefully.
engine.safety_exercise: Activity: {minutes} min {type} ({intensity}) in the last 4h — increased insulin sensitivity possible.
engine.engine_eval_spike: Post-meal glucose rose {delta} mg/dL — rapid spike beyond the {threshold} mg/dL threshold for {classLabel}.
engine.engine_eval_underdose: Post-meal glucose rose {delta} mg/dL — insulin insufficient for the carb load.
engine.engine_eval_overdose: Post-meal glucose dropped {delta} mg/dL — insulin exceeded glucose needs.
engine.engine_eval_good: Glucose stayed within ±30 mg/dL (Δ{delta}) — dose matched the meal.
engine.engine_eval_good_no_insulin: Glucose stayed within ±30 mg/dL (Δ{delta}) — trajectory ok without insulin.
engine.engine_eval_no_insulin_no_data: No insulin dose recorded — no comment possible without a follow-up reading.
engine.engine_eval_icr_overdose: Dose is {pct}% above the ICR-expected amount — likely over-dose (no post-meal reading).
engine.engine_eval_icr_underdose: Dose is {pct}% below the ICR-expected amount — likely under-dose (no post-meal reading).
engine.engine_eval_icr_good: Dose matches the ICR-expected amount within ±35% (no post-meal reading to confirm).
engine.engine_eval_hypo_during: Glucose dropped to {minBg} mg/dL inside the 3h post-meal window — hypoglycemia event despite any normal 2h reading.
engine.engine_eval_spike_peak: Post-meal glucose peaked {rise} mg/dL above baseline at +{peakAt} min — rapid spike beyond the {threshold} mg/dL threshold for {classLabel}.
engine.engine_eval_spike_speed: Glucose rose at {speed} mg/dL/min over the {window} window — speed beyond the {threshold} mg/dL/min threshold for {classLabel}, even though the 2-hour reading came back down.
engine.engine_speed1_rose: BG rose at {speed} mg/dL/min in the first hour.
engine.engine_speed1_fell: BG fell at {speed} mg/dL/min in the first hour.
engine.engine_speed2_rose: BG rose at {speed} mg/dL/min over the 2-hour window.
engine.engine_speed2_fell: BG fell at {speed} mg/dL/min over the 2-hour window.
engine.engine_ctx_high_activity: High activity today: {steps} steps vs. {avg} avg — insulin sensitivity may be increased. Context only.
engine.engine_lc_outside_window: Reading outside expected window (actual gap: {gap} min) — outcome cannot be reliably determined.
engine.engine_lc_provisional_1h_prefix: {window}: Δ {delta} mg/dL. Preliminary direction only — updates after the 2-hour reading.
engine.engine_chip_provisional_note: Note: the 2H reading is the final outcome indicator.
engine.engine_chip_trend_stable: Trend: BG stable in the early window.
engine.engine_chip_trend_falling: Trend: BG falling — provisionally stable or dropping.
engine.engine_rec_below_safety: Current glucose {bg} mg/dL is below the safety floor ({floor}). Treat the low first; avoid additional insulin.
engine.engine_rec_no_dose: No carbs and BG within target — no reference value calculated.
engine.engine_rec_clamped: Clamped to safety ceiling of {max}u — verify carb count or consult clinician.
engine.engine_rec_stacking: ⚠ {count} bolus doses in the last 6h — active insulin may still be working.
engine.engine_rec_exercise: ⚠ {minutes} min {type} ({intensity}) {hours}h ago — increased insulin sensitivity possible.
engine.engine_rec_high_activity: ℹ Today's activity is high ({steps} steps vs. {avg} avg). Insulin sensitivity may be increased — context only, dose unchanged.
engine.engine_rec_trend_rising_fast: Pre-meal trend: glucose rising fast (>1.5 mg/dL/min) in the last 15 min — dose unchanged, but watch closely.
engine.engine_rec_trend_rising: Pre-meal trend: glucose rising slightly (>0.5 mg/dL/min) in the last 15 min.
engine.engine_rec_trend_stable: Pre-meal trend: glucose stable in the last 15 min.
engine.engine_rec_trend_falling: Pre-meal trend: glucose falling slightly (>0.5 mg/dL/min) in the last 15 min.
engine.engine_rec_trend_falling_fast: Pre-meal trend: glucose falling fast (>1.5 mg/dL/min) in the last 15 min — check for hypo risk.
engine.engine_rec_trend_overshoot_warn: ⚠ BG just above target and rising fast — the correction may overshoot if glucose drops on its own shortly.
engine.engine_eval_trend_rising_fast: Pre-meal trend: glucose rose fast (>1.5 mg/dL/min) in the 15 min before the meal — part of the post-meal rise was already underway.
engine.engine_eval_trend_rising: Pre-meal trend: glucose rose slightly (>0.5 mg/dL/min) in the 15 min before the meal.
engine.engine_eval_trend_stable: Pre-meal trend: glucose stable in the 15 min before the meal.
engine.engine_eval_trend_falling: Pre-meal trend: glucose fell slightly (>0.5 mg/dL/min) in the 15 min before the meal.
engine.engine_eval_trend_falling_fast: Pre-meal trend: glucose fell fast (>1.5 mg/dL/min) in the 15 min before the meal — outcome may be influenced by that drop.
engine.bolus_sheet_adaptive_detail: {samples} meals · outcomes weighted (GOOD 1.0 · SPIKE 0.7 · over-/under-dose 0.3) · last 90 days
engine.bolus_sheet_static_detail: Manually configured ICR from your settings{slot}.

engineLog.default_now_hint: Default: now. Up to 365 days back-datable.
engineLog.link_suggestion_body: This meal sits inside the ±30 min window: {meal}. Link it directly?
engineLog.insulin_disclaimer: Glev doesn't calculate doses — you log what you injected. The CGM value is pulled automatically on submit.
engineLog.exercise_disclaimer: The CGM reading at log time helps Glev correlate exercise with glucose responses.
engineLog.footer_note_body: Insulin and exercise logs are pure documentation. Glev doesn't calculate doses or make insulin recommendations — that's between you and your diabetes team.
engineLog.exercise_logged_no_cgm: Logged — {minutes} min {type} ({intensity}){when}. No CGM reading available.
engineLog.adjustment_dismiss_hint: We'll hide this suggestion for 14 days.
engineLog.cycle_end_hint: Leave empty if bleeding is still ongoing.
engineLog.cycle_disclaimer: Glev does not predict anything and does not replace medical advice — entries are pure documentation for you.
engineLog.symptom_disclaimer: These entries are purely for personal journaling. They are not used for calculations or evaluations.
engineLog.symptom_logged_ok_with_cgm: {count} symptom(s) at severity {severity} saved · glucose {cgm} mg/dL.
engineLog.symptom_category_pms_hint: PMS symptoms surface as a luteal-phase signal in Insights.
engineLog.symptom_logged_pms_ok_with_cgm: {count} PMS symptom(s) at severity {severity} saved · glucose {cgm} mg/dL.
engineLog.influence_disclaimer: These entries are purely for personal journaling. They are not used for calculations or evaluations.

history.title: History
history.entries_subline: {shown} of {total} entries. Tap a row to expand.

insights.tile_raw_icr_explain: Naive average of carbs ÷ insulin over the last 7 meals. Ignores whether the dose actually landed in target — spikes and overdoses count the same as good outcomes. The Adaptive Engine ICR above is the smarter, outcome-weighted version.
insights.tile_avg_glucose_explain: Average pre-meal glucose. Lower reflects better fasting control.
insights.tile_good_rate_explain: Share of meals where the dose was within ±35% of the ICR estimate.
insights.tile_avg_insulin_explain: Mean insulin per meal. Track against carbs to validate your ratio.
insights.pattern_balanced_explanation: {pct}% of your last {n} meals landed in target — keep it up.
insights.pattern_overdosing_explanation: {pct}% of your last {n, plural, one {meal ended} other {# meals ended}} below target — your insulin is consistently too strong for these meals.
insights.pattern_underdosing_explanation: {pct}% of your last {n, plural, one {meal ended} other {# meals ended}} too high — your insulin is consistently too weak for your carbs.
insights.pattern_spiking_explanation: {pct}% of your last {n, plural, one {meal caused} other {# meals caused}} a rapid rise. Consider a pre-bolus or fewer fast carbs.
insights.pattern_insufficient_data_explanation: At least 5 meals with a post-meal reading are needed to detect a pattern (currently {n, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}}).
insights.card_glucose_trend_empty: Not enough data in this period for a trend yet.
insights.range_too_short_hint: Too little data for this period — try a longer range.
insights.engine_meals_in_range: {n, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}} in this period · engine learns from 90 days
insights.hypo_back_p3_clusters: Consecutive readings below 70 mg/dL are merged into one event (gap ≤ 15 min). Episodes shorter than 15 min count as a mini hypo, episodes ≥ 15 min as a longer event (specialist standard as orientation).
insights.card_time_in_range_empty: Log meals with pre-meal glucose to see your time-in-range.
insights.card_meal_evaluation_empty: Log meals with post-meal glucose to see your distribution.
insights.header_subtitle: Tap any card to flip · hold to reorder · {n, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}} analyzed
insights.tir_back_p1: Time in Range is the share of pre-meal glucose readings inside the 70–180 mg/dL consensus target band for adults with type 1 diabetes.
insights.tir_back_p2: Buckets follow the ATTD consensus (Battelino et al., Diabetes Care 2019): Very low (<54), Low (54–69), In range (70–180), High (>180). Spending more time in range is consistently linked to better long-term outcomes.
insights.tir_back_p3: Computed from {n, plural, one {# pre-meal reading} other {# pre-meal readings}} in the {range} window. The delta vs {prev} reflects movement against the previous comparable period.
insights.tir_empty: Log meals with pre-meal glucose to see your time-in-range.
insights.tir_detail_tbr_body: You spent {pct}% of the {range} window (~{hours} h) below 70 mg/dL. Guideline value (DDG/ADA): <4% (≈1 h/day). Detected: {mini} mini-hypo(s) <15 min and {clinical} longer hypo(s) ≥15 min.
insights.tir_detail_tir_body: You spent {pct}% of the {range} window (~{hours} h) between 70 and 180 mg/dL. International consensus target for T1D adults: ≥70% (Battelino et al., Diabetes Care 2019). Each extra hour in range lowers long-term complication risk.
insights.tir_detail_tar_body: You spent {pct}% of the {range} window (~{hours} h) above 180 mg/dL. Guideline value (DDG/ADA): <25%.
insights.tir_detail_now_body: {bg} mg/dL · {zone} · measured {ago} min ago. The marker shows your position on the 7-day bar above.
insights.page_medical_disclaimer: This analysis is for personal documentation only and does not replace medical advice.
insights.avg_bg_back_p1: Mean pre-meal glucose across the {range} window, calculated only from meals with a logged pre-meal reading.
insights.avg_bg_back_p2: Lower values reflect better fasting and overnight control. The delta vs {prev} surfaces movement against the previous comparable period.
insights.avg_bg_back_p3: Computed from {n, plural, one {# reading} other {# readings}} in the {range} window.
insights.gmi_back_p1: GMI (Glucose Management Indicator) approximates lab A1C from your average glucose. Formula: GMI(%) = 3.31 + 0.02392 × avg BG (mg/dL) — Bergenstal et al., Diabetes Care 2018.
insights.gmi_back_p2: A useful interim signal between clinic A1C draws — but not a substitute. Real A1C captures longer-term glycation that GMI cannot, and individual differences in red-blood-cell turnover can shift the two apart.
insights.gmi_back_p3_with_avg: Computed from your {range} window of pre-meal readings (avg {avg} mg/dL across {n}).
insights.gmi_back_p3_no_avg: Computed from your {range} window of pre-meal readings.
insights.trend_back_p1: Average pre-meal glucose for each day in the {range} window. Days without data inherit the previous day's value so the line stays continuous.
insights.trend_back_p2: Look for a flat line in your target range (70–180 mg/dL) and steady morning values. A rising slope over multiple days suggests it's time to revisit your basal or ICR.
insights.readings_window_p3: Computed from {n, plural, one {# reading} other {# readings}} in the {range} window.
insights.min_readings_window_required: At least {min} readings in the {range} window are required to show this card.
insights.hypo_back_p1: Number of glucose readings below 70 mg/dL in the {range} window. Pooled from meals, insulin and exercise logs plus manual fingersticks.
insights.hypo_back_p2: 70 mg/dL is the common orientation threshold for hypoglycaemia (specialist standard, ATTD consensus 2019). Frequent hypos are a good topic for your next clinician conversation.
insights.hyper_back_p1: Number of glucose readings above 250 mg/dL in the {range} window. Pooled from meals, insulin and exercise logs plus manual fingersticks.
insights.hyper_back_p2: Readings above 250 mg/dL count as marked hyperglycaemia (ATTD consensus 2019). Frequent hyper events can point to an ICR that is too high, a bolus that is too low or basal that is too low.
insights.meal_eval_back_p1: Each logged meal is bucketed into one of three outcome bands once the post-meal glucose lands: On target (within ±35% of the ICR estimate), Spiked (post-meal high), and Low risk (post-meal low).
insights.meal_eval_back_p2: Spike-heavy weeks often signal under-dosing or pre-bolus timing issues. Low-risk-heavy weeks often signal over-dosing — review your correction factor with your clinician.
insights.meal_eval_back_p3: Computed from {n, plural, one {# evaluated meal} other {# evaluated meals}} in the {range} window.
insights.meal_eval_empty: Log meals with post-meal glucose to see your distribution.
insights.engine_final_meals: {n, plural, one {# final meal} other {# final meals}}
insights.engine_icr_source: Based on {total, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}} from the past weeks
insights.engine_icr_source_tooltip: Glev uses your recent meals to calculate your personal carb ratio. Meals where you logged insulin alongside the meal count the most. For separate insulin entries, Glev tries to match them to the right meal automatically.
insights.engine_icr_relink_intro: Glev guessed that this insulin shot belongs to this meal. Is that right?
insights.engine_icr_relink_hint: Confirming helps Glev learn for next time.
insights.engine_icr_relink_failed: Could not save — please try again.
insights.engine_back_body: The Adaptive ICR is based on every completed meal (state = final, bg_2h present). Each meal is weighted by outcome: meals with a healthy BG response carry more weight than spikes or underdoses. It shows which carb-to-insulin ratio empirically led to stable readings for you — not what you dosed, but what actually worked.
insights.engine_msg_low_confidence: Pattern detected but confidence is too low to suggest changes.
insights.engine_msg_overdosing: Based on recent meals, your carb ratio could move from 1:{fromIcr} → 1:{toIcr} and your correction factor from {fromCf} → {toCf} (gentler dosing).
insights.engine_msg_underdosing: Based on recent meals, your carb ratio could move from 1:{fromIcr} → 1:{toIcr} and your correction factor from {fromCf} → {toCf} (stronger dosing).
insights.engine_msg_spiking: Spike pattern detected — consider pre-bolusing 10–15 min earlier rather than changing your ratios.
insights.engine_msg_hypo_warning: You had a low glucose event in {rate}% of your meals within the 3-hour window.
insights.engine_msg_fast_spike_hint: Your glucose peaks on average at {avgMinutes} min — an earlier pre-bolus may help.
insights.engine_msg_late_dip_warning: {rate}% of your meals showed a late glucose dip between 1–3 h after eating.
insights.tdd_back_p1: Total Daily Dose (TDD) is the sum of all logged insulin units per day — bolus + basal from the engine log.
insights.tdd_back_p2: Headline number: daily average across the {range} window (sum ÷ {days}). Today's running total is shown separately below. A steady TDD signals a stable metabolic baseline; swings > 20% can point to a changing insulin need.
insights.tdd_back_p3: Computed from {logs} insulin logs across {days} days in the {range} window.
insights.tdd_back_p3_insufficient: At least {min} days with insulin logs in the {range} window are required to show this card.
insights.patterns_back_p1: Glev scans the most recent 10 meals plus your time-of-day breakdown looking for repeating signals: consistent under-dosing, frequent over-dosing, strong recent control, weak mornings or strong evenings.
insights.patterns_back_p2: Patterns only fire when there's enough recent data — log 15+ meals to unlock the full set of detectors.
insights.patterns_back_p3: These flags are heuristics, not diagnoses. Use them as starting points for conversations with your clinician.
insights.pattern_under_dosing_desc: {n} of your last {total} meals were under-dosed. Consider increasing your ICR ratio or checking carb counts.
insights.pattern_over_dosing_desc: {n} of your last {total} meals led to over-dose. Review correction factor — it may be too aggressive.
insights.pattern_strong_control_desc: {n} of your last {total} meals were well-dosed. Your current insulin strategy is working.
insights.pattern_morning_issues_desc: Morning meals have a lower success rate. Dawn phenomenon may be increasing insulin resistance.
insights.pattern_evening_strength_desc: Evening meal dosing is particularly accurate. Use evening meals as reference for ICR calibration.
insights.pattern_no_signals_desc: Log 15+ meals to activate pattern detection. More data reveals deeper insights.
insights.pattern_mixed_desc: Your last 10 meals don't show a clear trend — wins and outliers balance out. This is normal; keep logging to surface real patterns.
insights.live_chart_stale_desc: Last point {age} ago — outside the 4h view. Reconnect sensor or tap refresh.
insights.workout_outcomes_back_p1: Glev classifies every workout from your glucose trace: STABLE = BG stayed in range. DROPPED = BG fell noticeably but no hypo risk. SPIKED = BG rose unexpectedly. HYPO_RISK = BG dropped under 70 mg/dL or got critically close.
insights.workout_outcomes_back_p2: PENDING sessions (CGM readings not yet in) are not counted. The headline number includes PENDING — the distribution below shows only classified workouts.
insights.workout_outcomes_back_p3: {total} workouts in the {range} window, of which {classified} have been classified.
insights.workout_outcomes_back_p3_insufficient: At least {min} workouts in the {range} window are required to show this card.
insights.workout_bg_response_back_p1: Shows how different workout types affect your average blood glucose — measured from before the session to one hour after. Helps surface which activities need BG adjustments.
insights.workout_bg_response_back_p2: At least 3 sessions per workout type with complete CGM readings (before and +1h) are required, otherwise the row is hidden.
insights.workout_bg_response_back_p3: {n, plural, one {# workout type} other {# workout types}} with enough data.
insights.workout_bg_response_back_p3_insufficient: No workout type has reached 3 sessions with complete CGM readings yet.
insights.workout_patterns_back_p1: Patterns automatically detected from your workout data. At least 3 sessions per group (time of day, workout type, duration) are required. Patterns refresh with every new workout.
insights.workout_patterns_back_p2: A pattern only appears when an outcome (STABLE / DROPPED / SPIKED / HYPO_RISK) dominates at least 60% of a group's sessions. PENDING sessions don't count.
insights.workout_patterns_back_p3: Currently {patterns} patterns from {evaluated} classified workouts in the {range} window.
insights.daily_steps_back_p1: Per-day step counts pulled from Apple Health on iOS. The card only appears once your iPhone has synced at least one day. Steps for today grow throughout the day, so the number above can change as you move.
insights.daily_steps_back_p2: The 7-day average gives you a baseline. Days more than 30% above average are highlighted as high activity — useful context for spotting why insulin sensitivity feels different today.
insights.daily_steps_back_p3: This is context only. Glev does not adjust your dose based on steps — talk with your diabetes team before changing your strategy around very active days.
insights.active_day_outcomes_back_p1: Pairs each logged meal with the same day's step count from Apple Health, then compares the GOOD / SPIKED / LOW RISK split on your most active days against your typical days.
insights.active_day_outcomes_back_p2: An "active" day is one with at least {threshold} steps (≈ 30% above your personal median). The card only appears once you have at least 14 days of step data and at least 3 classified meals on each side.
insights.active_day_outcomes_back_p3: This is an observation, not a recommendation. If the split looks very different on active days, bring it to your diabetes team — they can help decide whether your strategy around movement needs adjusting.
insights.active_day_outcomes_headline_flat: Your on-target rate looks similar on active and typical days ({active}% vs {typical}%).
insights.active_day_outcomes_headline_better: On active days your meals land on-target {delta} pp more often ({active}% vs {typical}%).
insights.active_day_outcomes_headline_worse: On active days your meals land on-target {delta} pp less often ({active}% vs {typical}%).
insights.active_day_outcomes_hypo_more: Low-risk meals also run {delta} pp higher on active days.
insights.active_day_outcomes_hypo_less: Low-risk meals run {delta} pp lower on active days.
insights.meal_type_back_p1: Glev classifies every meal into one of four macro profiles — Fast Carbs, High Protein, High Fat, or Balanced — based on the ratio of carbs, protein and fat.
insights.meal_type_back_p2: Success % is the share of meals in that category that landed in the GOOD outcome band. Categories with low success often need a different bolus strategy (timing, split dose, extended bolus).
insights.meal_type_back_p3: Categories with no logged meals are shown empty — log at least one meal of that type to see numbers.
insights.time_of_day_back_p1: Meals are grouped by the hour of day they were logged: Morning (5–11), Afternoon (11–17), Evening (17–21), Night (21–5).
insights.time_of_day_back_p2: Success % is the share of meals in that window that landed GOOD. A weak window (e.g. mornings <50%) often points at the dawn phenomenon, where insulin sensitivity is lower and you may need a higher morning ICR.
insights.time_of_day_back_p3: Strong windows (>80%) are reliable references when you're calibrating your dosing for new foods.
insights.empty_state_min_meals: Log at least 5 meals to see insights.
insights.cv_back_p1: Coefficient of variation (CV %) = (standard deviation / mean) × 100, computed from all glucose readings in the last 14 days. Shows how stable or volatile your glucose runs — independent of the absolute level.
insights.cv_back_p2: ATTD consensus 2019: < 36 % counts as stable (green), 36–50 % as medium (yellow), > 50 % as unstable (red). Higher CV values correlate with more frequent hypos.
insights.cv_back_p3: Computed from {n, plural, one {# reading} other {# readings}} in the last 14 days.
insights.cv_back_p3_insufficient: At least {min} readings in 14 days are required to show this card.
insights.disclaimer_default: Always discuss ICR adjustments with your endocrinologist.
insights.disclaimer_threshold: Thresholds are common orientation values. Always discuss deviations with your endocrinologist.
insights.raw_icr_info_body: The Raw ICR is the simple average of your last 7 doses — independent of whether the outcome was good or bad. It reflects your actual recent dosing behaviour. If this value diverges sharply from the Adaptive ICR, it can mean you have dosed differently than your long-term average lately — an observation, not a recommendation.
insights.raw_icr_info_subline: Source: last 7 meals with carbs + insulin · unweighted
insights.workout_pattern_desc: often leads to {outcome} ({pct}% of {n, plural, one {# session} other {# sessions}})
insights.cycle_symptoms_back_p1: An overview of your cycle and symptom entries from the {range} window. Pure documentation — no predictions, no medical assessment.
insights.cycle_symptoms_back_p2: Top symptoms are ranked by frequency; the dots show the average severity.
insights.symptom_top_empty: No symptom entries in the {range} window yet.
insights.swipe_default_title: Tap or swipe to see more
insights.swipe_default_body: Swipe horizontally to move between insights. A short explanation appears here for each card.
insights.swipe_ctx_time-in-range_body: Clinical benchmark: ≥ 70 % in range and < 4 % below 70 mg/dL. A stable or slowly rising number over several weeks is the goal — a sudden drop of several points is a signal to review basal, ICR, or bolus timing with your care team. The definition and how it's computed live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_gmi-a1c_body: Watch the trend between lab visits: is your number moving toward the personal target your care team set with you? A noticeable gap between GMI and your most recent lab A1c is worth raising at your next appointment — don't base settings changes on it alone. Formula and source live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_glucose-trend_body: Compare the line against what was happening in your life — travel, illness, cycle, training changes. Repeated multi-day swings are worth raising with your diabetes team before you adjust any settings yourself. Methodology and reading tips live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_hypo-events_body: Frequent or clinical (Level-2, < 54 mg/dL) hypos are a clear signal to talk to your diabetes team about basal, ICR or correction factor — not a cue to adjust on your own. Pay extra attention to nighttime events and hypos after exercise. Definition and sources live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_hyper-events_body: One-off spikes after special meals are normal. Recurring highs at the same time of day or after the same meal type are a pattern worth raising with your team — dose changes should never happen without clinical input. Threshold and sources live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_glucose-variability_body: A falling number means more predictable days and fewer hypo surprises — even when your average stays the same. A sudden rise is worth looking at against new routines (meal timing, exercise, stress) and discussing with your team. Formula and consensus bands live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_meal-evaluation_body: Compare your spike and hypo-risk rates across weeks where routines changed (exercise, travel, cycle phase). High rates are a good conversation starter with your diabetes team — not a reason to adjust bolus or factor on your own. Classification and methodology live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_adaptive-engine_body: Compares the carb-to-insulin factor the engine has learned against your currently configured value. For larger gaps it makes sense to observe the recommendation across a few days first, or to discuss it with your diabetes team, before adopting it permanently. How the value is computed lives on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_tdd_body: Track the trend of your total daily dose after changes (basal adjustment, new insulin type, seasonal shift, puberty, pregnancy). Sudden multi-day shifts are a discussion point for your diabetes team — don't base self-adjustments on this alone. Definition and calculation live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_patterns_body: Treat detected patterns as an observation hypothesis, not a directive: confirm them across several weeks, check influencing factors like sleep, stress or cycle, and bring stable patterns to your next clinic visit. How Glev derives patterns lives on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_workout-outcomes_body: Look at the distribution across different training types: many HYPO-RISK outcomes after long cardio or many RISES after intense strength training are typical T1D patterns to discuss with your team — ideally one experienced with exercise. Outcome definitions live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_workout-bg-response_body: Spot which workout types reliably pull you down or up — and which are unpredictable. This is good input for a conversation about pre-workout snacks, bolus reduction or basal adjustments with your diabetes team. Data requirements live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_workout-patterns_body: Stable training patterns are a good starting point for individual strategies around exercise — concrete implementation (snack size, bolus reduction, basal profiles) belongs with your team. How a pattern emerges and which thresholds apply live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_meal-type_body: Meal types with a low success rate across several weeks are a good prompt to discuss strategies like pre-bolus, split-bolus or extended bolus with your team — self-experimenting at large doses can cause hypos. Classification lives on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_time-of-day_body: Time-of-day differences show when your metabolism needs more or less insulin. A consistently weaker window across several weeks is a typical topic for your next diabetologist visit — factor or basal-profile changes belong there. Window definitions live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_cycle-symptoms_body: Compare symptom clusters and cycle phases against your glucose patterns in the same week. Recurring correlations are valuable input for a conversation with your gynecology or diabetes team — this card itself does not diagnose. Content and limits live on the back of the card.
insights.swipe_ctx_performance-tiles_body: Four metrics at a glance — useful for spotting trends after a settings change without diving into details. Tap a tile for the underlying formula. Bigger shifts are best discussed with your diabetes team.
insights.swipe_dyn_tir: Currently {pct}% in range · {delta} vs. previous period
insights.swipe_dyn_trend: Avg {avg} mg/dL · {delta} mg/dL vs. previous period

settings.title: Settings

settings.page_subtitle: Manage your profile and Glev settings.
settings.historical_data_desc: Replace your meal log with the 15 historical entries from the tracking sheet (Apr 17–22, 2026). Useful for resetting the app to a known demo state.
settings.historical_confirm: This will replace ALL your meal entries with the historical sample data (Apr 17–22, 2026). Continue?
settings.icr_hint: e.g. 15 = 1 unit per 15g carbs. Decimals allowed (e.g. 8.5).
settings.icr_engine_suggestion: Engine suggestion: 1:{value} · based on {n, plural, one {# meal} other {# meals}}
settings.icr_engine_no_data_yet: Engine has no suggestion yet — log a few meals with insulin.
settings.icr_auto_apply_hint: When on, the engine will automatically copy its suggestion into your carb ratio once it has 10+ meals of data. Default: off.
settings.target_bg_hint: Used by the dose recommender as the BG you want corrections to land on. Typically the midpoint of your target range (e.g. 110).
settings.sheet_dia_body: Duration of Insulin Action (DIA) determines how long your insulin stays active and drives the IOB calculation. 60–180 min = ultra-rapid profile, 180–240 min = standard rapid (NovoRapid, Humalog), 240–360 min = regular insulin.
settings.notif_critical_desc: Push on lows (<70 mg/dL) and highs (>250 mg/dL). Delivered immediately outside quiet hours.
settings.notif_smart_soon: Coming soon — learns your usual meal times and nudges you.
settings.notif_quiet_desc: Non-critical pushes are paused inside this window.
settings.about_me_sheet_desc: Glev uses these to tailor recommendations to you. Sex and birth year are required — height and weight are optional.
settings.about_me_birth_year_invalid: Please enter a valid year between {min} and {max}.
settings.about_me_height_invalid: Height must be between 50 and 280 cm.
settings.about_me_weight_invalid: Weight must be between 20 and 400 kg.
settings.about_me_optional_hint: Height and weight can be left blank.
settings.cycle_logging_desc: Shows "Log cycle" in the top-right "+" menu. Off by default — only visible when you enable it here.
settings.daily_macros_desc: Powers the "Today's Macros" rings on the dashboard. Defaults are sensible Type-1 starting points — adjust to match your nutrition plan. Saved to your account, syncs across devices.
settings.google_sheets_desc: Sync meals, insulin, and CGM into a spreadsheet.
settings.google_sheets_footnote: Connect your Google account to automatically mirror your Glev data into your own Google sheet. Comes after the CSV/PDF export feature.
settings.footer_disclaimer: Glev is a documentation and organization tool, not a medical device. Discuss treatment decisions with your physician.
settings.time_format_hint: "Automatic" shows 24h for German and AM/PM for English. Can be overridden manually for either language.
settings.sheet_units_body: Glev currently operates exclusively in mg/dL. A switch to mmol/L is planned for a future update.
settings.sheet_dexcom_body: Dexcom G6/G7/One+ on iPhone: the Dexcom app writes readings to Apple Health — Glev reads them automatically. Alternatively use Nightscout (all platforms).
settings.carb_unit_hint: How should carbohydrates be displayed? Calculations and storage stay in grams — only the display changes.
settings.low_alarm_hint: Glev fires an alarm as soon as your CGM reading drops below this threshold. The alarm repeats at most once every 15 minutes.

settings.adjustment_history_empty: No engine adjustments yet — once you accept a suggestion in the Engine tab, it shows up here.
settings.insulin_brand_body: Your brand name is shown in the IOB footer and pre-fills the insulin log form.
settings.insulin_brand_bolus_2_body: Optional — for users who alternate between two rapid-acting insulins (e.g. Fiasp for meals, NovoRapid as backup).
settings.basal_window_body: How long does your basal insulin stay active? Controls the countdown ring in the IOB panel. Pick your insulin brand or set the value freely (12–72 h).
settings.historical_intro: Resets your meal log to the historical demo entries (Apr 17–22, 2026). Useful for bringing Glev back to a known state.
settings.last_appointment_hint: Save the date of your last appointment. The Export panel will then show a one-click "Since last appointment" preset for exactly that window.
settings.appointments_hint: Keep a log of your doctor visits. The Export panel automatically uses the most recent one as the start date and lets you pick older ones from a menu.
settings.appointments_empty: No appointments saved yet. Add one above to enable the "Since last appointment" preset in Export.
settings.appointments_picker_hint: Choose an earlier doctor appointment as the start date for your export.

settings.subtitle_founder_contact: As a Glev+ member you have direct access to Lucas.

appleHealth.description: Read your glucose values directly from Apple Health — no matter which sensor writes them (Libre 3, Dexcom G7, Accu-Chek …). On first connect, iOS asks for permission once. After that, Glev pulls new values automatically while the app is open.

appleHealth.connect_only_ios: Apple Health is only available in the iOS app.

appleHealth.connect_success_no_values: ✓ Connected — no values found in Apple Health yet.
appleHealth.connect_sync_failed: Connected, but the initial sync failed.
appleHealth.backfill_description: The first sync only pulls the last 30 days. This button has Glev reach back through your older Apple Watch workouts in 90-day chunks — so the engine has a broader pattern base right away. Running this multiple times is safe (duplicates are detected server-side).
appleHealth.backfill_only_ios: Available only in the iOS app.
appleHealth.backfill_no_permission: Access to Apple Health not allowed.

appleHealth.backfill_success_with_count: ✓ Imported {inserted} older workouts (up to approx. {days} days back).
appleHealth.backfill_success_empty: ✓ No further workouts found in Apple Health (checked up to approx. {days} days back).
appleHealth.workouts_range_empty: Workouts: none synced from Apple Health yet.
appleHealth.permission_revoked: Apple Health access missing — please enable in iOS Settings

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle_count: {n} entries logged. Swipe horizontally through each cluster · tap to flip.
dashboard.iob_bg_hint: Contextual estimate — not a substitute for medical advice.
dashboard.adapt_score_tooltip: Only evaluated meals count — pending meals are excluded. Requires ≥ 3 evaluated meals. Formula: Good% × 0.7 + (100 − Spike% − Hypo%) × 0.3.
dashboard.control_score_back_body: The Adapt Score summarises how well your insulin doses matched your meals last week — the higher, the more precise.
dashboard.control_score_formula: Score = Good% × 0.7 + (100 − Spike% − Hypo%) × 0.3, last 7 days.
dashboard.control_score_explain: Rewards correctly dosed meals, penalises over- and under-doses. Badges: STRONG ≥ 80 · GOOD ≥ 60 · BUILDING < 60.
dashboard.iob_history_back_body: The curve shows active insulin (IOB) over the last 12 or 24 hours — each bolus contributes according to its activity profile.
dashboard.iob_history_back_how: IOB is calculated using the DIA triangle model: after an injection, insulin effect briefly rises then falls linearly to zero by the end of DIA (duration of insulin action). All active boluses are summed.
dashboard.rate_triplet_back_title: What do the three rates mean?
dashboard.rate_triplet_back_body: Good = dose matched the meal (within ±35% of the ICR ideal). Spike = too little insulin, glucose rose too high. Hypo = too much insulin, glucose dropped too low.
dashboard.rate_triplet_back_how: Calculated from evaluated meals in the last 7 days. Pending meals (no 2h reading yet) are excluded.
dashboard.good_explanation: The percentage of meals where your insulin dose was in the optimal range — neither too high nor too low.
dashboard.good_interpretation: Target >70%. Each GOOD outcome means your dose was within ±35% of the ICR-calculated ideal.
dashboard.spike_explanation: Meals where insulin was insufficient. Under-dosing leads to glucose spikes, which increase HbA1c long-term.
dashboard.spike_interpretation: Target <15%. Consistent under-dosing suggests your ICR or correction factor needs adjustment.
dashboard.hypo_explanation: Meals where the logged insulin was higher than the calculation suggested. This can be associated with lower glucose readings.
dashboard.hypo_interpretation: Reference value for your information. Discuss your factors with your clinician.
dashboard.outcome_basis: For your information: based on CGM readings 60–90 min after the meal.
dashboard.outcome_good_desc: Glucose within +30 mg/dL of the pre-value 1 h after the meal. The goal.
dashboard.outcome_under_desc: Glucose stayed clearly elevated — context for the conversation with your clinician.
dashboard.outcome_over_desc: Glucose dropped below the pre-value — orientation for your journal.
dashboard.outcome_spike_desc: Short spike above threshold — set the pre-bolus earlier.
dashboard.tip_no_meals: No meals logged yet today — add your first meal to start tracking.
dashboard.tip_all_on_track: All macros tracking close to target today — nice work.
dashboard.tip_lowest: {label} is at {pct}% of target — consider adding more in your next meal.
dashboard.no_entries_yet: No entries yet. Log your first meal.

entriesExpand.ex_disclaimer: For reference only — always consult your care team.
entriesExpand.basal_disclaimer: Basal is continuous — Glev does not score individual injections. For reference only.
entriesExpand.ex_waiting_at_end: Waiting for the at-end glucose reading…
entriesExpand.ex_hypo_share_text: of classified sessions ended in HYPO RISK
entriesExpand.type_explain_FAST_CARBS: High glycemic load, low fiber → fast absorption
entriesExpand.type_explain_HIGH_PROTEIN: High protein ratio slows glucose absorption
entriesExpand.type_explain_HIGH_FAT: High fat delays the glucose spike
entriesExpand.type_explain_BALANCED: Even macro distribution → stable glucose response
entriesExpand.eval_explain_HIGH: Insulin exceeded glucose requirements → glucose dropped significantly.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_OVERDOSE: Insulin exceeded glucose requirements → glucose dropped significantly.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_LOW: Insulin insufficient → glucose increased after meal.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_UNDERDOSE: Insulin insufficient → glucose increased after meal.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_SPIKE: Rapid glucose increase detected post meal.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_SPIKE_STRONG: Severe rapid glucose spike — magnitude or rate well above the threshold for this meal.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_CHECK_CONTEXT: Outcome unclear — review context before adjusting.
entriesExpand.eval_explain_HYPO_DURING: Glucose dropped below 70 mg/dL somewhere inside the 3h post-meal window — even if the 2h reading was back in range.
entriesExpand.lc_outside_window: Reading outside expected window (actual gap: {gap} min) — outcome cannot be reliably determined.
entriesExpand.lc_provisional_prefix: {window}: Δ {delta} mg/dL. Preliminary direction only — updates after the 2-hour reading.

marketing.faq_q1: What does Glev cost after the public launch?
marketing.faq_a1: From July 1, 2026, Glev Smart is available as the standard tier for €9/month. Starting now in Early Access locks in founder pricing that won't be available later — Glev Pro locks in €14.90/month as a lifetime price lock, Glev+ €29/month — both forever, even when regular prices go up later.
marketing.faq_a2: Available today: FreeStyle Libre 2 and 3 (via LibreLinkUp). Dexcom (G6, G7, One+) and Medtronic already work today via your own Nightscout instance (URL and token in settings). Apple Health (iPhone) is coming soon — along with the CGMs that would only run through Apple Health. A direct Dexcom and Medtronic integration is still in development.
marketing.faq_a3: No. Glev is a documentation and organization tool. Therapy decisions stay between you and your doctor.
marketing.faq_a4: In the EU (Ireland) on Supabase — we deliberately chose the EU region so all health and account data stays inside the European Union, with no replication to third countries. German GDPR. No data sharing, no advertising.

marketing.brand_foundation_what_text: The voice-first meal tracker for type-1 diabetics. Log meals in seconds, AI-estimated macros, glucose live from CGM.
marketing.brand_foundation_not_text: Not a medical device. Not an insulin calculator. Not a diabetes coach. Glev is a documentation and organisation tool — therapy decisions are made with your doctor.
marketing.brand_foundation_who_text: Type-1 diabetics with CGM (currently FreeStyle Libre 2 via LibreLinkUp; Dexcom and Nightscout in the works). Smartphone-native, German-speaking market first.
marketing.brand_foundation_quote: “Built by Lucas — T1D, because existing apps were too slow for me.”
marketing.brand_color_trend_note: Live glucose readings in the app use exactly this colour scheme — never map them differently. Hypo/hyper are status signals, not style; brand blue (#4F6EF7) stays in charge of generic accents.
marketing.brand_type_inter_note: Loaded via next/font/google in app/layout.tsx as the CSS variable --font-inter.
marketing.brand_type_mono_note: Reserved for: glucose readings, insulin units, timestamps, hex codes, IDs, table cells. Exposed as the CSS variable --font-jetbrains-mono, available project-wide via var(--font-mono).
marketing.brand_scale_body_sample: Log meals in seconds, AI-estimated macros, glucose live from CGM.
marketing.brand_logo_intro: The Glev mark is a node graph: 7 nodes, 10 edges, 1 central insulin atom. The geometry is fixed — only the foreground and background colour are tweakable.
marketing.brand_logo_primary_desc: Default variant. For all dark surfaces.
marketing.brand_logo_onbrand_desc: On brand blue for marketing backgrounds.
marketing.brand_logo_onlight_desc: For light print or web surfaces.
marketing.brand_logo_dont_1: Don't distort or rotate the geometry.
marketing.brand_logo_dont_2: Don't remove or recolour nodes or edges.
marketing.brand_logo_dont_3: Don't drop the mint dot from the wordmark.
marketing.brand_logo_dont_4: No drop shadow, no gradient, no outline stroke around the mark.
marketing.brand_voice_intro: Glev sounds like an experienced T1D who keeps it short. Direct, numerical, no pathos. German in the informal “du”, English in technical context (CGM, in-range, hyper, hypo).
marketing.brand_voice_dont_1: Your glucose readings are looking really pleasing right now!
marketing.brand_voice_dont_2: Awesome, we successfully captured your meal! 🎉
marketing.brand_voice_dont_3: Oops! Looks like there's a small issue with the connection.
marketing.brand_principle_short_text: At most 2 lines per UI string. Verbs before nouns.
marketing.brand_principle_numeric_text: Values first, explanation after. mg/dL and g carbs spelled out.
marketing.brand_principle_neutral_text: No emojis, no exclamation marks, no coaching tone.
marketing.brand_principle_honest_text: Never “calculate”, never “dose”. Glev documents — therapy is your doctor's job.
marketing.brand_compliance_no_med_title: Glev is not a medical device
marketing.brand_compliance_no_med_body: Glev is a documentation and organisation tool for people with type-1 diabetes. Glev does not calculate insulin doses, gives no therapy recommendations and is not certified under MDR/IVDR. You make therapy decisions with your treating doctor.
marketing.brand_compliance_data_text: Glucose, meal and insulin data live in Supabase (EU region). Auth via Supabase, AI features via Replit AI Integrations.
marketing.brand_compliance_cgm_text: FreeStyle Libre 2 via LibreLinkUp (read-only). Credentials encrypted in the DB; revocable from settings at any time.
marketing.brand_compliance_trademark_text: “Glev” and the Glev mark are a word mark and figurative mark. Use in press contexts is allowed without asking — please don't change the geometry or colours.
marketing.landing_founder_quote: "In April 2026 I was diagnosed with Type 1. In the first weeks I tested every popular T1D app — and none of them feels like it was built for daily life. Glev is the app I would have needed from day one."
marketing.faq_q5: Where do I find a setup guide for my CGM?
marketing.faq_a5: Step-by-step guides per brand are on the /setup page — Dexcom, FreeStyle Libre, Medtronic, Apple Health and Nightscout, each ready to go in under 5 minutes.
marketing.faq_q6: Does Glev work without a CGM?
marketing.faq_a6: Yes. You can type glucose values manually and log meals without a CGM integration. The adaptive engine learns more slowly with less data, but it works.
marketing.faq_q7: Do I need an iPhone for Apple Health?
marketing.faq_a7: Yes, Apple Health is iOS-only. Android users connect via LibreLinkUp (Libre) or Nightscout (all brands). A direct cloud integration with Dexcom and Medtronic is in development.
marketing.faq_q8: What data is sent to the AI?
marketing.faq_a8: Only your meal text. When you type e.g. "cheese sandwich with tomato", Glev sends exactly that one sentence to OpenAI so it can estimate carbs, protein and fat. Glucose values, insulin doses, your name or email are never transmitted — those stay encrypted on our server in the EU (Ireland). OpenAI is contractually prohibited from using these texts to train their models.

setupPage.hero_sub: Step-by-step guides for the popular sensors. Every brand, every method — ready to go in under 5 minutes.
setupPage.cta_title: Ready to use Glev with your CGM?
setupPage.cta_sub: Sign in and configure your sensor in Settings — the app walks you through the same picker described here.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_intro: Recommended on iPhone: the Dexcom app writes readings to Apple Health and Glev reads them from there. On Android or with your own infrastructure, Nightscout works as well.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_step_1: Open the Dexcom app (G6, G7 or One+) and confirm your sensor is actively sending readings.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_step_2: In iPhone Settings → Health → Data Access & Devices → Dexcom: allow writing of “Blood Glucose”.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_step_3: Install the Glev iOS app and sign in. On first launch we ask for the Health permission — confirm it.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_step_4: Done. Glev reads new readings in the background — typically every 5 minutes once the Dexcom app has synced.
setupPage.vendor_dexcom_note: A direct Dexcom API integration (without Apple Health) is in development. For now use Apple Health (iPhone) or Nightscout (all platforms).
setupPage.vendor_libre_intro: Recommended path: LibreLinkUp — works directly on the web and in the iOS app, without Apple Health or Nightscout. All you need is a follower account.
setupPage.vendor_libre_step_1: Open your LibreLink app (the sensor app, not LibreLinkUp). Tap “Connected Apps” → LibreLinkUp and add a person.
setupPage.vendor_libre_step_2: That person registers for free in the LibreLinkUp app with their own email + password and accepts the invite.
setupPage.vendor_libre_step_3: In Glev → Settings → CGM → FreeStyle Libre. Enter the LibreLinkUp account email + password and pick your region (EU/US).
setupPage.vendor_libre_step_4: Glev connects, fetches the latest readings, and syncs every minute from then on.
setupPage.vendor_libre_note: You can also create your own LibreLinkUp account and add yourself as a follower — no second person needed.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_intro: Medtronic sensors sync to Apple Health on iPhone (via the MiniMed Mobile app). Glev reads the values from Health.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_step_1: Open the MiniMed Mobile app and confirm your pump or sensor is connected and sending readings.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_step_2: In iPhone Settings → Health → Data Access & Devices → MiniMed Mobile: allow writing of “Blood Glucose”.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_step_3: Install the Glev iOS app and sign in. Confirm the Health permission in Glev on first launch.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_step_4: Glev reads the latest readings from Apple Health and syncs in the background from then on.
setupPage.vendor_medtronic_note: Direct Carelink integration is in development. Medtronic also works with Nightscout — that requires your own Carelink uploader instance.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_devices: Bridge for Dexcom · Libre · Medtronic · Eversense and more
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_intro: Apple Health is the central health-data store on iPhone. If your CGM app writes readings to Health — regardless of brand — Glev can pick them up there.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_step_1: Check in your CGM app (Dexcom, MiniMed, Sugarmate, Libre via xDrip4iOS, etc.) that readings are being written to Apple Health.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_step_2: In iPhone Settings → Health → Data Access & Devices → [your CGM app]: enable “Blood Glucose” as a written data category.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_step_3: Install the Glev iOS app. On first launch Glev asks for permission to READ “Blood Glucose” from Health — confirm it.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_step_4: Glev imports the last 24 h immediately, then checks for new data every few minutes.
setupPage.vendor_apple_health_note: Apple Health is iOS-only. On Android, use LibreLinkUp, Nightscout or your vendor's own cloud integration instead.
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_devices: Open source · all platforms · all CGM brands
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_intro: Nightscout is a self-hosted web bridge that writes readings from your CGM into your own database. If you already run Nightscout, hooking Glev into it is trivial.
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_step_1: Make sure your Nightscout instance is running and reachable at a public URL (e.g. https://myglev.nightscout.com).
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_step_2: Optional: in Nightscout under “Admin Tools” create an API token with the “readable” role — only needed if your instance is not publicly readable.
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_step_3: In Glev → Settings → CGM → Nightscout: enter your URL and (if used) the token.
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_step_4: Glev verifies the connection, fetches the latest reading, and syncs automatically from then on.
setupPage.vendor_nightscout_note: Nightscout is not for beginners — setting up your own instance takes 30–60 minutes depending on the host. If you already have one, you are done in under 2 minutes.

chips.eval_explain_LOW: Insulin insufficient → glucose increased after meal.
chips.eval_explain_HIGH: Insulin exceeded glucose requirements → glucose dropped significantly.
chips.eval_explain_OVERDOSE: Insulin exceeded glucose requirements → glucose dropped significantly.
chips.eval_explain_UNDERDOSE: Insulin insufficient → glucose increased after meal.
chips.eval_explain_SPIKE: Rapid glucose increase detected post meal.
chips.eval_explain_SPIKE_STRONG: Severe rapid glucose spike — magnitude or rate well above the threshold for this meal.
chips.eval_explain_HYPO_DURING: Glucose dropped below 70 mg/dL somewhere inside the 3h post-meal window — even if the 2h reading was back in range.
chips.eval_explain_CHECK_CONTEXT: Outcome unclear — review context before adjusting.
chips.type_explain_FAST_CARBS: High glycemic load, low fiber → fast absorption
chips.type_explain_HIGH_PROTEIN: High protein ratio slows glucose absorption
chips.type_explain_HIGH_FAT: High fat delays the glucose spike
chips.type_explain_BALANCED: Even macro distribution → stable glucose response
